title: Nutrients in food - Canada.ca
content:
Learn about the nutrients, fats and vitamins that are found on the nutrition facts table on packaged foods. Also learn how to use this information to make healthier food choices.

## Services and information
Learn about calcium, which foods to eat to help you get enough.
Learn to make healthier food choices by using the Nutrition Facts table on prepackaged foods. Carbohydrates are found in the Nutrition Facts table.
Learn about cholesterol and in which foods it is found. Also discover how its % daily value is calculated.
Find out about the different types of fats found in your food.
Get information on fibre and in which foods it can be found. Also learn how fibre’s % daily value is calculated.
Learn about iron, which foods to eat to help you get enough, how to increase iron absorption.
Learn about protein and which foods are high in this nutrient.
Get the basics about sodium and how to reduce it at the grocery store, at home and when eating out. Also use the Sodium Interactive Tool to detect sodium.
Learn about sugars and which foods have a lot of them.
Discover information on vitamin A, the foods it is found in and how to calculate its % daily value.
Learn about vitamin C, which foods to eat to help you get enough.
Learn about vitamin D, how to get enough from foods and supplements.
